title: NOC 14110 – Survey Interviewers and Statistical Clerks | GTR Immigration
description: Survey Interviewers and Statistical Clerks play a critical role in Canada’s data-driven economy by supporting research projects, census programs, government surveys, and business analytics. Classified under NOC 14110, this occupation has experienced growing demand across both public and private sectors since the pandemic, creating promising immigration pathways through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Growing reliance on data collection and analysisGovernment census and polling programsExpansion of private-sector market researchEntry-level TEER occupation with advancement opportunitiesEmployer-supported recruitment
classification.major_group: 141 – Office, court, and data support occupations
classification.minor_group: 1411 – Survey, statistical, and data entry occupations

duties: Contact individuals by telephone or in person to explain survey purposes
Record responses on paper or through computer-assisted interviewing systems
Check information for completeness and accuracy
Code responses according to established manuals
Enter data into statistical software programs
Compile interview results into reports and lists
employment_requirements: Driver’s license for certain field-based roles
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is expected to create approximately 11,000 new jobs for Survey Interviewers and Statistical Clerks, while about 10,900 new job seekers will be available—making employer sponsorship, provincial nomination, and strategic immigration planning especially important for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Survey Interviewers and Statistical Clerks are in demand across Canada’s private and public sectors. Hiring has expanded significantly since the pandemic and is expected to continue growing over the coming years.
Market research and polling firmsGovernment departments and agenciesUtility companiesContact centersStatistical research establishmentsCensus and election offices
Annual salaries typically range between $28,900 and $80,900 CAD, depending on experience, province, and specialization.
